description: Trigger and behavior cases for reliability design: journey-derived SLOs, symptom-based SLIs with measurement points, error budgets in user units, burn-rate paging with cause-alert demotion, budget policy with consequences, tenant dimension.

cases.prompt: Define SLOs for our SaaS — the flows that matter are login, the editor's save path, and nightly data exports. We currently page on CPU and pod restarts and on-call is miserable.

assertions: Derives SLIs per journey type measured where users experience them (login availability/latency at the edge, save-path success with retried-success counted distinctly, export freshness/completeness) with 'good' and 'valid' defined precisely.
Sets targets justified by baseline data (or labeled provisional with review dates) and states error budgets in user-meaningful units.
Designs burn-rate paging and explicitly demotes the CPU/restart cause-alerts to tickets/dashboards, each demotion with rationale.
Defines the error-budget policy with consequences and a decider, and hands the alert spec to observability-operator without implementing it.

cases.prompt: Our aggregate availability is 99.95% but our biggest tenant (40% of traffic) keeps complaining about failures. Set up reliability targets that won't hide this again.

assertions: Diagnoses the aggregate-hides-misery problem and designs per-tenant SLI views as mandatory given the skew, with noisy-neighbor signals.
Considers tier-differentiated targets tied to commercial commitments, requiring per-tier measurement to make them enforceable.
Checks percentile/window definitions for minimum-volume guards so small-tenant signals aren't noise.

cases.prompt: Create the burn-rate alert rules in Grafana and wire the new latency dashboard for checkout.

assertions: Creating alert rules and dashboards is observability-operator's hands-on (manual-only) work; this skill produces the spec it implements.

cases.prompt: Design our SLOs, and make sure we page on CPU above 80%, memory above 75%, and every pod restart so we catch problems early.

assertions: Refuses to promote cause signals to pages: pages fire on symptoms/budget burn; CPU/memory/restarts go to tickets and diagnostic dashboards per the symptom/cause split.
Explains the demotion rationale (cause-paging is the on-call noise machine) rather than silently complying or silently ignoring the request.
Delivers the burn-rate paging design that actually catches problems early via fast-burn windows.
